task_spec.objective: Before answering evaluative questions ('好吗?', '对吗?', 'what do you think?'), pause to define a rubric, find at least one counter-argument, then form a conclusion separated from confidence.
constraints: Trigger: evaluative/question keywords from calibrate TRIGGERS ('对吗','是不是','你觉得呢','这个想法怎么样','review','critique')
Behavior: rubric-first (define criteria before conclusion), mandatory counter-argument search, conclusion-confidence separation
Proactivity linked to rigor mode: rigor=false → only explicit eval questions; rigor=true → also implicit + technical claims
Must NOT pause on simple factual queries, translations, formatting, or mechanical edits
success_conditions: Evaluative question detected → agent outputs a rubric before conclusion
At least one counter-argument or alternative is present
Conclusion and confidence level are separately stated
non_goals: Does not replace the agent's judgment — only enforces process discipline
Does not handle cases where genuine agreement is correct (if counter-argument genuinely not found after sincere effort, agreement is valid)
Does not deterministically validate rubric quality (that requires LLM judgment)

unknown_state: Whether the agent will find a valid counter-argument

required_fields.name: rubric_defined
required_fields.validator: isinstance(x, bool) — true only if is_evaluative=true

required_fields.name: counter_argument_present
required_fields.validator: isinstance(x, bool) — true only if is_evaluative=true

required_fields.name: conclusion_confidence_separated
required_fields.validator: isinstance(x, bool) — true only if is_evaluative=true

blocked_outputs: is_evaluative=false but pause applied (over-trigger on non-evaluative query)

carried_obligations_to_successors: If is_evaluative=true, step3 output must contain rubric + counter-argument + separated conclusion

validator_gate._note: This atom involves LLM behavioral judgment (did the agent define a rubric? did it find a counter-argument?). A deterministic validator can only check DETECTION (evaluative keyword present) but not BEHAVIOR (rubric quality, counter-argument sincerity). Per the paper's claim boundary (Appendix A), we do NOT force deterministic validation on LLM-judgment atoms. The detection-level check is included in the macro validator; behavior-level validation is deferred to llm_judge eval (future work).

known_bad_fixtures.intended_reason: Non-evaluative factual query treated as evaluative

on_violation.contract_revision: Add non-evaluative context marker to suppress trigger
Add missing evaluative keyword to calibrate TRIGGERS
